steps.title: Determine what kind of sentence the blank must be
steps.content: The phrase "to find the answer" signals that Jenouvrier conducted a study to answer a question that was just posed. The blank must therefore form a direct question. A direct question in English requires two things: inverted word order (auxiliary verb before subject) and a question mark.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice and confirm the correct one
steps.content:
• Choice A — these penguins could become extinct.: Subject-before-auxiliary word order makes this a declarative statement, not a question; the period compounds the error. ✗
  • Choice B — these penguins could become extinct?: The question mark is present but the word order is still declarative ("these penguins could"); inversion is required for a grammatical direct question. ✗
  • Choice C — could these penguins become extinct.: The auxiliary "could" is correctly placed before the subject, but the period fails to mark the sentence as a question. ✗
  • Choice D — could these penguins become extinct?: Auxiliary "could" precedes the subject "these penguins" (correct inversion) and the question mark closes the direct question correctly. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
